title: Delete CloudKit records on resolution/expiry + purge action
description: Audit item 5, docs/AUDIT-2026-07.md. Neither side ever deletes records; expires_at is not a TTL.

title: Redact relay payload to companion-rendered fields
description: Audit item 4, docs/AUDIT-2026-07.md. payload_for_relay (mcp.rs:143) ships the whole AskRequest.

title: Decide relay fate: implement Mac-side answer ingest or gate the feature
description: Audit item 3, docs/AUDIT-2026-07.md. Companion submits answers to CloudKit but no Mac-side subscription/poll ingests them into registry.resolve; round-trip never completes. Default to disabled until it works.

title: Document all four network paths in SECURITY.md + README
description: Audit item 2, docs/AUDIT-2026-07.md. Updater, Apple speech model download, HuggingFace TTS model download, CloudKit relay when enabled — current docs claim the updater is the only exception.

title: Relay opt-in: default iphone routing Off + companion-sync setting + entitlement/config coherence
description: Audit item 1, docs/AUDIT-2026-07.md. RoutingConfig::default() sets iphone: Fallback (routing.rs:66,90) while the shipped build's entitlement is removed — make relay opt-in with first-run disclosure and a visible 'not available in this build' state.

title: Panel timed out mid-typing and discarded user's text (data loss)
description: User reported: typing into a prompt text field, the panel timed out and closed, losing the typed input. Fix (0.3.2): keep-alive floors the prompt deadline on every focus/input (KEEPALIVE_EDIT_S=600) and relaxes to 45s on blur; floor is enforced both in the webview hide-timer (App.tsx, self-rescheduling against max(budget, interactionFloor)) AND server-side (registry.rs Pending.min_deadline + effective_deadline(), resettable wait loop, new keepalive command/Tauri cmd) so a delivered answer still resolves; in-progress text also saved to localStorage draft as a safety net. Requirement from user: NEVER close an actively-edited window; reset timeout to >=45s after editing stops; never lose user data.
close_reason: Fixed in 0.3.2: keep-alive deadline (webview + server-side registry) + draft safety net.

title: 0.3.0 macOS build bricked: restricted iCloud entitlements without provisioning profile (AMFI SIGKILL)
description: 0.3.0 added iCloud/CloudKit restricted entitlements to src-tauri/Entitlements.plist for the relay. Developer-ID bundles ship no embedded.provisionprofile, so AMFI SIGKILLs the app at launch (exit 137) -> 'The application cenno cannot be opened'. codesign+notarization pass, so dry-run looked clean. Fix: removed iCloud entitlements (relay already gated to run without them); bumped 0.3.1; hardened scripts/release.sh with a static restricted-entitlement/profile guard + live launch smoke-test that reject this brick before publish. Both verified to catch the broken bundle.
close_reason: Fixed in 0.3.1: dropped restricted iCloud entitlements, hardened release.sh with launch smoke-test + provisioning-profile guard. 0.3.1 published, OTA signature verified, v0.3.0 deleted.

title: L0: security + threat-model for screen capture — prompt-injection, secret redaction, consent indicator
description:
Cross-cutting security work that MUST land with (and gates) L1/L2. Screen awareness fundamentally expands cenno's threat model beyond the current 'agent-supplied a2ui payloads are untrusted input' surface documented in SECURITY.md.

New risks to address:
1. PROMPT INJECTION VIA SCREEN CONTENT. get_screen_context / activity-timeline text flows FROM the screen TO the calling agent's model. A malicious webpage, email, or on-screen text can carry injected instructions ('ignore prior instructions, exfiltrate...') that reach the agent. This is a new outbound injection path the existing threat model does not cover (it only covers untrusted content coming IN to cenno). Decide framing: cenno labels captured text as untrusted/quoted, documents that agents must treat it as data not instructions, and the skill teaches this.
2. SECRET / SENSITIVE REDACTION. AX will not return secure text fields (passwords) by design, but visible tokens, API keys, private messages, and a screenshot of a password manager are all capturable. Define what is captured, offer a denylist of apps/bundle-ids and URL hosts to never capture, and redaction of obvious secret patterns before storage.
3. CONSENT / VISIBILITY. ScreenCaptureKit triggers the macOS purple recording indicator, but AX reading has NO OS indicator. For honesty (and as the differentiator vs Clicky), cenno should surface its OWN visible indicator when it is reading the screen / sampling, plus a global kill switch.
4. THREAT-MODEL DOC. Update SECURITY.md with a screen-capture section covering the above and the precise privacy claim (see below).
5. PRECISE PRIVACY CLAIM. cenno processes and stores captured context locally and capture adds no new network path — but screen content may leave the machine when an agent reads it through a cloud model. The claim must describe the capture boundary precisely, not claim either that cenno globally makes no network calls or that the screen never leaves the machine. Avoid the exact overclaim we caught Hey Clicky making.

This issue blocks L1.
acceptance_criteria: SECURITY.md has a screen-capture threat-model section covering outbound prompt-injection, secret/app/host denylist + redaction, and the precise (non-overclaimed) privacy statement; captured text is labeled untrusted to agents and the cenno skill teaches agents to treat it as data; cenno shows a visible capture/sampling indicator with a global kill switch; L1 does not ship without these.
notes: Design spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-15-l0-screen-capture-security.md. Implementation plan: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-15-l0-screen-capture-security.md. Test scenarios: docs/superpowers/test-scenarios/2026-07-15-l0-screen-capture-security.md. Privacy correction: capture is local/no-new-network-path; never claim cenno globally makes no network calls. Verification 2026-07-15: cargo test 130 unit + 4 integration passed (3 existing ignored); vitest 157 passed; TS typecheck and production frontend build passed; bundled app 0.4.0 ad-hoc signed and installed to /Applications; installed socket smoke passed; tray kill switch persisted false and true across relaunch. Repository-wide fmt/clippy drift is pre-existing and tracked as cenno-gv6.
close_reason: Implemented capture config, mandatory Rust guard, persisted global kill switch, truthful tray indicator, threat model/privacy copy, agent trust rule, and reusable test scenarios; built, installed, and smoke-tested app.

title: L2: activity-timeline sampler (AX + AXObserver) + get_activity_timeline query
description:
Passive activity timeline: a rolling, local log of what the user has been doing, grounded in Accessibility data.

AX-FIRST + EVENT-DRIVEN: sample foreground context on AXObserver notifications (kAXApplicationActivatedNotification, kAXFocusedWindowChangedNotification, kAXValueChanged) rather than polling on an interval — wake only on context change. Per sample capture app_bundle_id, app_name, window_title, site_host (browser URL host via AX address-bar read), and a short context_summary. This fills most of Hey Clicky's activity-timeline.sqlite columns cheaply and entirely on-device. cenno already reads CGWindowListCopyWindowInfo in suppress.rs (window titles/bounds) — AX is the next tier up (content + semantics + events).

deep_context (the long LLM-generated summary Clicky stores) is the ONE piece cenno cannot do locally without a model — make it OPTIONAL and agent-driven: an agent can enrich rows on demand via a tool, or leave them null. Store in cenno.db (0600, local-only), reuse db.rs conventions, add retention/pruning policy.

Query surface: MCP tool get_activity_timeline(since, until[, app]) and a cenno CLI subcommand (cenno timeline / extend cenno export) so an agent or the user can answer 'what was I working on this morning'.

Depends on L1 for the shared AX/Swift bridge.
acceptance_criteria: AXObserver-driven sampler writes {app,window_title,site_host,context_summary} rows to cenno.db on context change (no interval polling); rows queryable via get_activity_timeline MCP tool and a CLI subcommand; deep_context is optional/agent-enriched; retention policy prunes old rows; cenno stores samples locally; agent queries may be sent onward by the requesting agent.
notes: AUDIT CORRECTION (Fable): 'AXObserver-driven' is only half right. Foreground APP-SWITCH events come from NSWorkspace.didActivateApplicationNotification, NOT AXObserver (the system-wide AX element does not reliably post focus notifications). WITHIN-app focus/value changes come from a per-app AXObserver you must register on each running app (add on app launch, tear down on quit). So the sampler = NSWorkspace for app-level changes + per-app AXObserver for in-app changes. Browser URL/site_host via AX address-bar read is FRAGILE (Chrome needs AXManualAccessibility/AXEnhancedUserInterface toggling which can slow the browser; Safari more reliable) — consider Apple Events/AppleScript as an alternative for URL, and treat site_host as best-effort.

title: L1a: get_screen_context MCP tool — AX-only (focused app/window/URL/selected+visible text)
description:
New MCP tool get_screen_context, AX-ONLY path (the cheap, high-value core; capture+OCR fallback is split out to L1b).

Read what the user is looking at via the macOS Accessibility API and return it as structured text to the calling agent — no pixels, no OCR, on-device, instant:
- Focused app (name + bundle id) and focused window title, from a system-wide AXUIElement -> AXFocusedApplication -> AXFocusedWindow.
- Focused UI element: role/subrole, AXValue (text field/area contents), AXSelectedText + selection range.
- Visible text of the focused window where cheaply available (AXStaticText / AXValue / AXStringForRange over the visible range) — bounded in size.
- Browser URL / site_host when the frontmost app is a browser (best-effort; see L2 note on AX address-bar fragility — Apple Events fallback acceptable).

Return shape: structured JSON as MCP text content (app, bundle_id, window_title, url?, role, selected_text?, visible_text?, truncated flag). Bound payload size and validate at the boundary (protocol/a2ui_guard conventions).

Degrade gracefully: if Accessibility permission is not granted, return a typed 'permission_denied' result (not an error) and trigger the TCC prompt via AXIsProcessTrustedWithOptions(kAXTrustedCheckOptionPrompt). If the AX tree is empty/thin (Electron/canvas/video), return a typed 'ax_unavailable' result so the agent (and later L1b) knows to fall back — do NOT capture pixels in this issue.

Implementation: Swift (AXUIElement) bridged via swift-rs, extending the voice.rs -> swift/ SpeechTranscriber pattern. cenno is non-sandboxed so AX-client access is allowed.

Blocked by L0 (security/threat-model). Shipping this alone is useful: 'what's in my focused window right now' with zero image/OCR cost.
acceptance_criteria: get_screen_context returns focused app+bundle_id+window_title+role and, where available, selected_text/visible_text/url from AX as MCP text content on native apps; permission-denied and ax-unavailable are typed results (not errors); Accessibility TCC prompt is triggered lazily; payload size is bounded and validated; NO pixel capture or OCR in this issue; the screen-context path adds no cenno network call and captured content is labeled untrusted before return.

title: Context-awareness: AX-first screen awareness + proactive nudges
description:
Extend cenno from a reactive ask-channel into a bidirectional, screen-aware assistant, inspired by teardown of Hey Clicky (com.humansongs.clicky). Direction flip: screen -> agent (context), and agent -> proactive nudge, while keeping captured context locally processed/stored and adding no new cenno network path; connected/scheduled agents supply intelligence and may use remote models.

Design principle across all children: ACCESSIBILITY-FIRST. Read the macOS Accessibility API (AXUIElement) for focused app/window/URL/selected-text/visible-text and use AXObserver events for change detection. Fall back to ScreenCaptureKit + on-device Vision OCR (VNRecognizeText) only when the AX tree is empty (Electron, canvas apps, video, images). cenno is non-sandboxed (hardened runtime, Developer-ID) so it is eligible to be an AX client once the user grants Accessibility permission (separate TCC gate from Screen Recording).

Implementation pattern: do AX/capture in Swift and bridge via swift-rs, extending the existing voice.rs -> swift/ SpeechTranscriber pattern rather than pulling AXUIElement into Rust via objc2.

Prior art reference (Hey Clicky, from local bundle inspection): ProactiveMonitor/ActivityTimelineTracker Swift classes; ~/Library/Application Support/Clicky/activity-timeline.sqlite (app_bundle_id, app_name, window_title, site_host, context_summary, deep_context); throttling via ProactiveCooldownMinutes/ProactiveMaxCardsPerDay; NO local OCR (ships screenshots to cloud vision). cenno's differentiator: on-device capture and preprocessing with explicit agent-boundary labeling, vs Clicky's cloud proxy + 'no passive recording' marketing that the binary contradicts.

Children: L1 get_screen_context (on demand), L2 activity-timeline sampler + query, L3 proactive surfacing, L4 optional context logging for analytics.

title: tray 'Show pending prompt' is unreachable-dead: suppression guard + dismiss semantics leave no state where it acts
description:
replay_pending() (lib.rs:399) is the handler for the tray item (tray.rs:199) and the reopen shortcut. Every reachable prompt state makes it a silent no-op:

1. Prompt parked while paused/fullscreen — the ONE state the item exists for — is blocked by the suppression guard at the top of replay_pending ('replay skipped — still suppressed'). An explicit user click should override suppression.
2. Dismissed (✕ or dismiss_pending MCP): registry.dismiss() consumes the tx, so pending() excludes it — yet the menu item's own comment (tray.rs:95) promises recovery 'after it was dismissed'. Not resurrectable by design (agent already got TimedOut) — comment/menu copy is wrong.
3. Timed out: deadline passed → excluded from pending().
4. Still on screen: replay re-emits the same prompt — no visible change.

An unsuppressed pending prompt is always already shown (arrival emit + schedule_panel_show; pause-expiry timer and fullscreen-toggle-off both auto-replay), so the 'hidden but pending and unsuppressed' state the item could serve essentially never exists.

Proposed fix: add force flag to replay_pending that bypasses the suppression check for the explicit tray click / global shortcut; fix the tray.rs:95 comment re dismiss; optionally disable the menu item (or retitle) when pending() is empty so the no-op is visible.

title: Sync panel display with TTS readiness; cancel speech on answer/dismiss
description:
When voice output is enabled, the panel currently appears before TTS audio is generated, leaving a noticeable gap between the panel showing and speech starting. Sometimes the user answers and closes the panel before playback even begins — the voice then plays for an already-dismissed prompt.

Desired behavior:
1. When a prompt will be spoken, defer showing the panel until the TTS audio is ready (or starts playing), so panel + voice arrive together. Keep a fallback: if synthesis exceeds a short deadline (~2-3s), show the panel anyway rather than delaying the prompt indefinitely.
2. Cancel/stop synthesis and playback the moment the prompt is answered or dismissed — never speak for a closed panel.

Likely touch points: frontend tts_speak call flow (PromptPanel.tsx / useTtsPlayer), tts.rs + supertonic synthesis path, and the show-panel sequencing in lib.rs/bridge.rs.
close_reason: Implemented, live-tested on a release build, merged via PR #8: deferred panel-show handshake (panel_ready + 2.5s fallback), cancel-safe Supertonic stop via generation gate, 200ms fade-out on stop.

title: Audit P1 batch: retention+VACUUM, CI, model hash pinning, registry eviction, cancellation, single-instance, relay consistency
description: Items 6-12 of docs/AUDIT-2026-07.md improvement list (Codex-audited). Split into tasks when picked up.

title: Voice-out (sound-out) silent: panel gates on startup config snapshot, not live config
description: Repro: enable/retune voice-out in cenno settings while the app is already running, then trigger a prompt -> no speech, even though settings 'test voice' works. Root cause: the prompt panel resolves the TTS gate (shouldSpeak) from getTts(), populated once at startup via the get_user_config command (the launch snapshot). Settings test-playback reads fresh (read_config_file) AND bypasses the gate, so it always works -> masks the bug. tts_speak itself already reads config fresh per call, but the GATE did not, so every prompt after the user turned voice-out on was silently dropped until an app restart refreshed the snapshot (verified live: a restart made prompts speak). Fix: useTtsPlayer now re-reads tts config fresh from disk (new userConfig.readFreshTts -> read_config_file) per prompt before gating, so settings changes take effect on the next prompt with no restart. Also added: 'cenno' -> 'chenno' phonetic respelling in normalizeForSpeech (Italian pronunciation). Frontend-only; ships in next release.

title: CloudKit relay crashes app on unentitled/dev build
description: cenno_relay_write_prompt -> makeDB() builds CKContainer(iCloud.app.cenno).privateCloudDatabase. On an ad-hoc/unsigned local build (no icloud-container-identifiers entitlement), CloudKit raises an ObjC exception outside any do/catch -> SIGTRAP, whole app crashes (faultingThread in cenno_relay_write_prompt closure). Repro: companion routing targets non-empty + a prompt published on a locally-built app. Fix: gate relay FFI on iCloud entitlement presence so it no-ops when CloudKit unavailable.
close_reason: Gated relay FFI on iCloud entitlement (SecTaskCopyValueForEntitlement); no-ops when CloudKit unavailable instead of trapping

title: TestFlight: verify full prompt loop on Production build
description: After aps-environment=production + Production CloudKit schema deploy, prove write -> device displays prompt -> answer flips record to answered, on a Production/Distribution build via TestFlight. Gate before submitting for review.
notes: Build 1.0(1) uploaded to App Store Connect / TestFlight successfully 2026-06-18 (xcodebuild Upload succeeded, processing). Path: archive CennoiPhone -> exportArchive app-store-connect with Admin API key KY2K3HP233. REMAINING (human device test): once the build finishes processing, install via TestFlight on a device signed into the SAME iCloud as the Mac writer (glebis+apple@gmail.com), trigger an MCP ask_user prompt, confirm it displays on Production CloudKit and answering flips the record to answered. This is the final gate before submitting for review.

title: App Store Connect: register bundle IDs + create app record
description: Register App IDs app.cenno.companion (+ watch) with iCloud(CloudKit) + Push Notifications capabilities, and create the App Store Connect app record. Reserve the store-facing name (check 'Cenno' availability; fall back to a qualified name if taken). Web/portal work.
notes: DONE 2026-06-16: App Store Connect record created. Store name 'Cenno' was TAKEN; used 'Cenno Companion' as store name (home-screen label stays 'Cenno' via CFBundleDisplayName). Apple ID 6780882240, SKU cenno-companion, iOS, English (U.S.), Bundle ID app.cenno.companion, Full Access. App ID capabilities (iCloud/CloudKit/Push) already verified.
close_reason: App Store Connect app record created (Apple ID 6780882240, store name 'Cenno Companion', bundle app.cenno.companion). Name 'Cenno' was taken; home-screen label remains 'Cenno'.

title: App Store distribution signing: cert + provisioning profiles
description: Ensure an Apple Distribution certificate exists and App Store provisioning profiles cover app.cenno.companion + app.cenno.companion.watch with the iCloud/CloudKit + push capabilities. CODE_SIGN_STYLE is Automatic, so Xcode can mint these on Archive/Distribute; confirm or create manually in the Developer portal.
close_reason: Distribution signing PROVEN via successful App Store upload. xcodebuild -exportArchive (method app-store-connect, automatic signing, -allowProvisioningUpdates) minted the Apple Distribution cert + App Store provisioning profiles for app.cenno.companion + .watch and signed the build. Required a NEW Admin-role App Store Connect API key (KY2K3HP233) — the pre-existing Developer-role key (8S89DZRB8R) lacked cloud-signing permission. Archive scheme: CennoiPhone (embeds Watch+Shared). Build 1.0(1) uploaded OK 2026-06-18.

title: Deploy CloudKit schema to Production environment
description: Indexes (state/expires_at QUERYABLE, created_at SORTABLE) auto-create only in Development on first save. Must explicitly Deploy Schema Changes -> Production in CloudKit Dashboard or queries return empty for shipped users. Verify record types/zone Prompts exist in Production.
close_reason: Deployed Prompt record type + 19 indexes (state/expires_at queryable, created_at sortable) + role perms to Production CloudKit via dashboard Deploy Schema Changes. Verified: Production env now lists Prompt (13 fields). Additive deploy, no data loss.

title: Switch aps-environment development -> production
description: iPhone entitlement currently pins aps-environment=development; TestFlight/App Store builds need production APNs + Production CloudKit. Update CennoiPhone.entitlements (and any watch equivalent). This is the top relay blocker for real users.
close_reason: Per-config entitlements: Debug=development, Release=production aps-environment. Added CennoiPhone.Release.entitlements, switched iPhone target to per-config CODE_SIGN_ENTITLEMENTS in project.yml, regenerated + verified via showBuildSettings.

title: Finalize Cenno companion (iPhone + Watch) for App Store release
description: Epic tracking everything needed to ship the CennoCompanion iPhone + Watch apps to TestFlight/App Store. The relay loop is proven only in Development CloudKit; production path (Production CloudKit + Distribution signing + App Store Connect records + metadata) is unstarted. See memory cenno-cloudkit-relay-delivery.

title: Restore repository-wide rustfmt + strict clippy gates
description: The L0 screen-capture verification run found pre-existing repository-wide quality-gate failures unrelated to L0. cargo fmt --check reports formatting drift across much of src-tauri; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings reports six warnings in generation.rs, registry.rs, and supertonic/helper.rs. Fix in a dedicated mechanical change so security work stays reviewable.
acceptance_criteria: cargo fmt --check passes; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings passes; cargo test remains green; formatting-only changes are isolated from behavior changes.

title: L1b: get_screen_context fallback — ScreenCaptureKit + on-device Vision OCR / image return
description:
Extend get_screen_context (from L1a) with a visual fallback for when AX returns 'ax_unavailable' (Electron, canvas apps like Figma, video, images) or when the caller explicitly requests visual mode.

- Capture the focused window (preferred) or display via ScreenCaptureKit (SCScreenshotManager / SCStream one-shot). Requires Screen Recording TCC (NSScreenCaptureUsageDescription) — request lazily, typed permission_denied result on refusal. Note ScreenCaptureKit shows the macOS purple recording indicator (good for consent; complements L0's own indicator).
- On-device OCR via Vision (VNRecognizeTextRequest, accurate level) -> return recognized text (with optional per-line bounding boxes for future 'point at it' use).
- Optionally return the screenshot itself as MCP IMAGE content (rmcp 1.7 Content supports image) so the agent's own vision model reads it — caller chooses text|image|both via a tool arg. Downscale before returning (respect the downscale-for-task convention; cap max dimension ~1568 like Clicky's set-of-marks driver) to bound tokens.
- Do NOT proxy to any cloud vision API — the capture/OCR path adds no cenno network call; intelligence is the agent's.
- Apply L0's denylist/redaction (never capture/return for denylisted apps/hosts) before OCR or image return.

Implementation: same Swift/swift-rs bridge as L1a (add ScreenCaptureKit + Vision). Blocked by L1a.
acceptance_criteria: When AX is unavailable or visual mode is requested, get_screen_context captures the focused window via ScreenCaptureKit and returns OCR text and/or a downscaled screenshot image (rmcp image content) per a caller arg; Screen Recording permission is requested lazily with a typed denial; L0 denylist/redaction is applied before OCR/image return; images are downscaled to bound tokens; no cloud vision proxy; the capture/OCR path adds no cenno network call and agent-bound content remains explicitly untrusted.

title: L6: persistent collapsible per-agent windows (opt-in 'buddy' mode)
description:
Optional persistent, collapsible window per live agent — the spatial manifestation of the L5 agent roster, inspired by Hey Clicky's always-present control panel ('an ai buddy that lives next to your cursor').

DESIGN DECISION (make it explicit, do not skip): this partly contradicts cenno's founding metaphor 'fare un cenno' — a brief sign then gone. The ephemeral, non-activating, one-question-then-dismiss panel MUST remain the DEFAULT. Persistent windows ship as an OPT-IN mode (tray + ~/.cenno/config.json), not a replacement. Adopted as default, persistent chrome would turn cenno from a nod into a dashboard and dilute its identity. Treat this issue as carrying a real identity tradeoff, not just a UI add.

Behavior:
- One window per live agent, driven by the L5 roster: appears when an agent connects, closes when it disconnects.
- Collapsed state = a small pill/dot (agent label + status badge); expanded = current in-flight ask, recent activity, and status. Collapse/expand toggled by the user; state + window position persisted per-agent across app restarts.
- Non-activating (reuse the existing NSPanel no-focus-steal machinery); Tauri multi-window. Handle stacking/positioning of multiple agent windows and multi-display.
- Natural home for proactive nudges (L3): a nudge badges or expands the agent's existing window instead of sliding in a new unsolicited panel — less intrusive. Respect the same quiet-mode/fullscreen/cooldown governance from suppress.rs.

Depends on L5 (agent roster) for which windows exist and their state; interacts with L3 (proactive surfacing) as an alternate, gentler surface.
design: Opt-in mode; ephemeral panel stays default. Per-agent window lifecycle bound to L5 roster. Reuse non-activating NSPanel + Tauri multi-window. Persist collapse-state + position per agent. Proactive nudges (L3) surface via badge/expand rather than a new panel.
acceptance_criteria: With the opt-in mode enabled, each live agent (per L5 roster) gets a non-activating, collapsible window that appears on connect and closes on disconnect; collapsed shows label+status, expanded shows current ask/activity; collapse-state and position persist across restarts; the default (mode off) keeps the ephemeral panel behavior unchanged; proactive nudges can surface via the agent's window; quiet-mode/fullscreen governance still applies.

title: L5: agent-awareness — track live agents (herdr-style), expose via MCP + CLI
description:
Make cenno aware of which agents are currently running/connected, the way herdr is aware of its running agent panes — so context and proactive nudges can be attributed and routed per agent, and the user can see what's live.

Scope:
- Track connected MCP client sessions on cenno's server (identity/label, connect time, last-activity, in-flight ask). cenno already records 'via' on each answered prompt in history — extend to a live roster, not just post-hoc attribution.
- Optionally ingest external agent state: read herdr's running-agent view (HERDR_ENV / herdr CLI over its unix socket) and/or Claude Code session presence, to show agents that are running but not currently connected over MCP.
- Expose a roster: MCP tool (e.g. list_agents) returning {label, source (mcp|herdr|session), state, last_seen, current_task?} and a CLI subcommand (cenno agents).

Uses: route a proactive nudge (L3) to a specific agent; show in tray which agents are live; let an orchestrator see the fleet. Keep it read-only/observational and local; no control plane in this issue.

Relates to L3 (routing nudges per agent) and L4 (per-agent analytics).
acceptance_criteria: cenno maintains a live roster of connected MCP client sessions (label, connect/last-activity, in-flight ask); optionally surfaces herdr/Claude Code running agents; roster is queryable via a list_agents MCP tool and a 'cenno agents' CLI subcommand; observational only; local-only.

title: L3: proactive surfacing — scheduled companion agent + cenno as delivery/governance
description:
The flip: cenno proactively surfaces something without the user asking. cenno has no LLM, so it does NOT decide — it provides the context source + the surface + the etiquette; an external scheduled agent (reusing the user's existing cron / /loop infrastructure) is the brain.

Loop: a scheduled Claude Code (or similar) reads get_activity_timeline + get_screen_context, decides there's something worth saying, and calls ask_user to surface a nudge card. cenno's job = delivery + GOVERNANCE: enforce cooldowns, per-day caps, quiet mode, and fullscreen suppression. Much of this policy already exists in suppress.rs (fullscreen detection, quiet mode, queue/replay) — extend it with proactive-specific throttling (cooldown minutes, max cards/day) mirroring Clicky's ProactiveCooldownMinutes / ProactiveMaxCardsPerDay knobs, exposed in ~/.cenno/config.json.

Design questions to resolve in this issue: how a proactive/unsolicited prompt is marked vs a normal ask_user (new field or flag), how cenno rate-limits an over-eager agent at the boundary, and how dismissal/acceptance is recorded (feeds L4 analytics). Keep cenno dumb-by-design; the intelligence lives outside.

Depends on L1 (screen context) and L2 (timeline) as the context inputs.
acceptance_criteria: A scheduled companion agent can read context tools and raise an unsolicited nudge through cenno; cenno enforces cooldown + max-cards/day + quiet-mode + fullscreen suppression on proactive prompts (config in ~/.cenno); proactive prompts are distinguishable from normal ask_user; dismiss/accept outcomes are recorded; cenno itself makes no autonomous LLM/network calls.

title: L4: optional context logging for analytics, queryable via MCP + CLI
description:
Make cenno optionally persist the AX/screen context stream (from L1/L2) as the user's own local analytics dataset — same ethos as 'your answers become your own queryable dataset', extended from Q&A history to activity/context.

OPT-IN and OFF BY DEFAULT: logging is a user toggle (tray + ~/.cenno/config.json). When on, cenno retains the activity-timeline samples (and optionally enriched summaries) for longer-horizon analysis; when off, samples are used transiently for L1/L3 and not retained. Make the retention window and what-fields-are-logged configurable. cenno stores retained context locally in cenno.db (0600); a querying agent may send results onward to its model provider.

Query/analytics surface:
- MCP: get_activity_timeline (shared with L2) plus aggregate helpers (e.g. time-per-app, per-site, per-window rollups) so an agent can do reflection/analytics.
- CLI: cenno export (extend existing exporter) with timeline + rollups (json/csv) for the user's own scripts, and a summary subcommand.

Privacy: document clearly in SECURITY.md what is captured when logging is enabled; provide a one-shot purge command (cenno timeline --purge). This is the honest local-first counter to Clicky's cloud-summarized activity log.
acceptance_criteria: A user-facing opt-in (default off) controls whether context samples are retained; when on, timeline + aggregate rollups are queryable via MCP tools and via cenno export (json/csv) and a summary CLI; retention window and logged fields are configurable; a purge command wipes retained context; SECURITY.md documents the capture surface; cenno stores retained context locally and discloses that querying agents may send results onward.

title: tell_user MCP tool — one-way outbound voice/panel notification (no answer)
description: First wedge of the general-assistant direction (docs/BACKLOG.md 2026-07-13). ask_user minus the answer plumbing: agent delivers a spoken/panel message, composes with existing urgency gating, quiet mode, and TTS. No new privacy surface.

title: Audit P2 batch: link allowlist, corrupt-DB resilience, repo hygiene, glue tests, dep tracking, pending badge
description: Items 13-18 of docs/AUDIT-2026-07.md improvement list (Codex-audited). Split into tasks when picked up.

description: SettingsWindow.tsx: drop the live About/version line.

title: Settings: configurable shortcut field, persist to ~/.cenno/config.json
description: SettingsWindow.tsx field + config.rs schema. Default suggestion Cmd+Shift+C. Validate at boundary.
close_reason: Closed

title: Global shortcut: add tauri-plugin-global-shortcut, register configurable combo
description: Cargo.toml dep, lib.rs .plugin() + register from config, capabilities/default.json perms. Fail soft. Calls replay_pending.
close_reason: Closed

title: Tray item: 'Show pending prompt' calls replay_pending
description: src-tauri/src/tray.rs: add MenuItem with_id 'show_pending' (~L96) + match arm calling crate::replay_pending(app) (~L162).
close_reason: Closed

title: Draft restore: read cenno-draft-<id> back into the field on prompt re-show
description: Frontend. App.tsx active-change effect (~L231): read localStorage, write value + dispatch input so a2ui binds. Scope to text/textarea. Restore once per id; don't clobber active typing. Cleared on answer/dismiss already.
close_reason: Closed

title: Panel text is never lost
description: Draft restore + reopen pending prompt (tray + configurable global shortcut). Goal: docs/factory/2026-06-24-panel-text-never-lost/goal.md

comments.author: glebis
comments.text: Implemented + 3-round codex audit (all findings closed: cross-prompt leak via session-nonce keys, empty-clear, keepalive-on-restore, startup-order panic, NUL byte). Built+installed 0.3.2 locally, restarted. Pending: user interactive verification, then commit/merge.

title: Pre-existing: tests/mcp_socket.rs fails to compile (start_socket_server signature drift)
description: cargo test (integration) fails: tests/mcp_socket.rs calls start_socket_server with args not matching its current signature (E0061/E0277, ~24 errors, e.g. passing Option where impl Fn() expected). Unrelated to the keepalive change (lib + unit tests compile and pass). Blocks 'cargo test' full run; use 'cargo test --lib' meanwhile. Update the integration test to the current start_socket_server signature.

title: cenno voice-flow primitive: external answer + dismiss handle
description: Agent-driven voice Q&A (cenno speaks question, user answers via external STT like Spokenly) needs the panel to persist while the user answers, then dismiss the moment the external answer lands. Today cenno exposes only ask_user (blocks on in-panel answer) — no external resolve/dismiss. Add: (a) MCP/CLI to dismiss or answer a prompt by id, OR (b) a 'speak + show self-card' notify mode that auto-dismisses on a follow-up call. Surfaced live while wiring cenno-voice + Spokenly. Also: Spokenly mutes playback while recording, so speak must fully finish before the mic opens (handled by adaptive gap); panel timeout-based hide fires too early.
notes: dismiss_pending MCP tool landed (commit 403e88b) — the external dismiss handle works end-to-end (validated live with cenno voice + Spokenly). Remaining: a fully internal voice-flow mode so dismissal is instant (no agent round-trip), and adaptive speak/listen timing baked in.

title: Review /cenno skill: update + voice support
description: Audit skills/cenno (SKILL.md + scripts) for staleness and ensure it documents/supports voice-out (sound-out): say summaries, urgency gating, engine=supertonic, voice selection F1-F5/M1-M5.
close_reason: Added urgency/say to ask_user ref, Voice-out section, tts config block + interview question

title: Main settings window (tabbed: Settings/Integration/About)
description: Add a normal decorated 'settings' window opened from the tray. Tabs: Settings (Voice/TTS engine+voice+test, behavior toggles incl launch-at-login, hide-from-dock; defaults flow/theme), Integration (MCP config snippet + copy, shell one-liner, skill install), About (what cenno is, GitHub/LinkedIn/repo links, version). First prototype.

title: Honor tts.model_path (custom model path precedence + validation)
description: supertonic::model_dir uses config.tts.model_path if set+valid, else default cache, else AVSpeech fallback. Bad path never crashes.
close_reason: model_dir honors tts.model_path (valid→custom, invalid→default→AVSpeech); ENGINE keyed by resolved dir; pick_model_dir unit-tested.

title: Voice-out settings UI (engine, voice picker, model block)
description: React SettingsView: engine toggle system/supertonic, Supertonic voice picker, model status + Download(progress) + custom path. Reads get_user_config, writes write_user_config.
close_reason: Settings window shipped + verified live: opens from tray, renders, reflects+writes config (no clobber — panel/widgets/routing preserved), engine/voice persist, model status shows installed, Supertonic test voice works (no fallback), output-device picker + delete-model wired.

title: Settings WebviewWindow + tray 'Settings…' item + label routing
description: Dedicated normal window (not NSPanel) opened from tray; frontend renders SettingsView when window label=settings, else prompt App.
close_reason: Settings window shipped + verified live: opens from tray, renders, reflects+writes config (no clobber — panel/widgets/routing preserved), engine/voice persist, model status shows installed, Supertonic test voice works (no fallback), output-device picker + delete-model wired.

title: write_user_config command (atomic, clobber-safe round-trip)
description: Read+merge+write ~/.cenno/config.json preserving all fields (widgets/panel/routing/defaults/tts). Atomic temp+rename. Round-trip test. Foundational + main clobber risk.
close_reason: Settings window shipped + verified live: opens from tray, renders, reflects+writes config (no clobber — panel/widgets/routing preserved), engine/voice persist, model status shows installed, Supertonic test voice works (no fallback), output-device picker + delete-model wired.

title: Settings surface + TTS model management (v1)
description: In-app settings (dedicated window from tray) with a Voice-out section: engine, voice picker, model download+progress, custom path. Adds config-write path. Goal: docs/factory/2026-06-22-settings-tts-model/goal.md.

title: Audio playback path for WAV (AVAudioPlayer or Rust sink)
description: Child of cenno-0wc (Supertonic on-device TTS). See docs/factory/2026-06-22-supertonic-tts/goal.md.
close_reason: Done in integration commit 372e1c3: synth→PCM (supertonic::synthesize), rodio playback (speak_blocking+stop), engine switch + AVSpeech fallback (tts_speak dispatch). Verified in-app: F3 spoke in cenno, no fallback, user confirmed match.

title: tts.engine switch + AVSpeech fallback on load/synth error
description: Child of cenno-0wc (Supertonic on-device TTS). See docs/factory/2026-06-22-supertonic-tts/goal.md.
close_reason: Done in integration commit 372e1c3: synth→PCM (supertonic::synthesize), rodio playback (speak_blocking+stop), engine switch + AVSpeech fallback (tts_speak dispatch). Verified in-app: F3 spoke in cenno, no fallback, user confirmed match.

title: Synthesize text→WAV via chosen path (English, default voice/quality)
description: Child of cenno-0wc (Supertonic on-device TTS). See docs/factory/2026-06-22-supertonic-tts/goal.md.

title: Supertonic model fetch + cache to app data (one-time, on enable)
description: Child of cenno-0wc (Supertonic on-device TTS). See docs/factory/2026-06-22-supertonic-tts/goal.md.
close_reason: Settings window shipped + verified live: opens from tray, renders, reflects+writes config (no clobber — panel/widgets/routing preserved), engine/voice persist, model status shows installed, Supertonic test voice works (no fallback), output-device picker + delete-model wired.

title: spike: choose Supertonic integration path (Swift SDK vs Rust ort) — build-weight + latency
description: Child of cenno-0wc (Supertonic on-device TTS). See docs/factory/2026-06-22-supertonic-tts/goal.md.
close_reason: Spike validated Rust ort 2.0 path: builds 32s, self-contained preprocessing (no g2p/subprocess), CPU. Latency=text-len x steps: short say @steps5 ~0.5s (meets target), long body @steps8 ~6s (too slow). Mitigation: speak short say + cap steps ~5-6. Assets ~380MB on disk.

title: sound-out: Supertonic on-device TTS backend (better voice, stays R1)
description: Integrate Supertonic (99M, ONNX, MIT, native Swift+Rust SDK; https://github.com/supertone-inc/supertonic) as a higher-quality on-device TTS backend behind tts_speak, with AVSpeechSynthesizer as fallback. Fully local — no cloud, no API keys, privacy stays R1 (unlike cloud providers cenno-fx5). Slots into existing CennoVoice Swift-FFI. Vault refs: ai-research/Technology/Supertonic.md. Size-matched alternatives if it disappoints: Kokoro (~82M, already used in Pipecat stack), PocketTTS, MeloTTS. Needs: model asset download/caching, voice selection config, Swift SDK wiring. Smallest slice = Supertonic English speaking the say/normalized text, fallback to AVSpeech on load failure.

title: sound-out: select a better on-device voice (Enhanced/Premium/Siri neural)
description: Quick win: default AVSpeechVoice sounds plain. Pick a high-quality installed voice (enhanced/premium/Siri) via AVSpeechSynthesisVoice, configurable in ~/.cenno tts.voice. Big quality jump, still on-device/no network.
close_reason: Shipped + verified by ear: say-field speaks short summary instead of body; auto-selected premium on-device voice. User approved sound.

title: sound-out: dedicated agent-provided 'say' text (short spoken summary)
description: GOAL AMENDMENT from live demo: reading the full title+body aloud is hard to listen to. Add optional AskRequest.say (or tts_text) that, when present, is spoken INSTEAD of the normalized body — a short summary the agent writes for the ear. Falls back to body when absent. Frontend useTtsPlayer prefers say when set.
close_reason: Shipped + verified by ear: say-field speaks short summary instead of body; auto-selected premium on-device voice. User approved sound.

title: Stop/skip control in panel chrome
description: Add a stop-reading affordance to the panel (like the dismiss x) that calls tts_stop. Advisory visual evidence at one viewport.
close_reason: v1 sound-out shipped: normalizer+gating (21 tests), config, Swift AVSpeechSynthesizer bridge, useTtsPlayer hook, mute control. Verified: 140 vitest + tsc + cargo config tests green.

title: Swift AVSpeechSynthesizer TTS bridge + Rust tts_play/tts_stop commands
description: On-device TTS: Swift CennoTts (AVSpeechSynthesizer) with C ABI entry points; Rust tts.rs module + Tauri commands tts_play(text)/tts_stop. No network.
close_reason: v1 sound-out shipped: normalizer+gating (21 tests), config, Swift AVSpeechSynthesizer bridge, useTtsPlayer hook, mute control. Verified: 140 vitest + tsc + cargo config tests green.

title: Prompt-shown hook + urgency gating (frontend)
description: useTtsPlayer hook: when active prompt mounts, if tts.enabled && urgency>=threshold (High always), normalize title+body and call tts_play. Deterministic, no double-fire. Gating unit-tested.
close_reason: v1 sound-out shipped: normalizer+gating (21 tests), config, Swift AVSpeechSynthesizer bridge, useTtsPlayer hook, mute control. Verified: 140 vitest + tsc + cargo config tests green.

title: Prompt text normalizer (markdown + code identifiers → speakable)
description: Pure function: strip/flatten markdown (**bold**, backticks, headings, lists) and voice code identifiers (paths like refactor/i5ly.4-split-db-rs, dotted filenames db.rs, dump_schema.rs) intelligibly. Must never drop body substance. TDD with the db-refactor example as a fixture. This is the highest-logic unit.
close_reason: v1 sound-out shipped: normalizer+gating (21 tests), config, Swift AVSpeechSynthesizer bridge, useTtsPlayer hook, mute control. Verified: 140 vitest + tsc + cargo config tests green.

title: TTS config schema (tts.enabled, tts.min_urgency)
description: Extend Rust Config/DefaultsConfig + frontend UserConfig with tts.enabled (bool, default false) and tts.min_urgency (low|normal|high, default high). Plumb through get_user_config.
close_reason: v1 sound-out shipped: normalizer+gating (21 tests), config, Swift AVSpeechSynthesizer bridge, useTtsPlayer hook, mute control. Verified: 140 vitest + tsc + cargo config tests green.

title: sound-out: voice-out (TTS) for cenno prompts
description: Optional on-device TTS that speaks ask_user prompts aloud, gated by urgency. Goal contract: docs/factory/2026-06-22-sound-out/goal.md. JTBD: ~/jtbd/sound-out/jtbd.json. v1 = on-device macOS AVSpeechSynthesizer only; cloud providers + ElevenLabs earcons + emotional markup are follow-on.

title: Embed Distribution profile + Production CloudKit in shipped cenno.app
description: For the relay to work in the DISTRIBUTED build: embed a Developer-ID/Distribution provisioning profile for app.cenno + iCloud.app.cenno provisioned for PRODUCTION, re-sign with full entitlement set (scripts/macos-cloudkit-sign.sh release path), and deploy the Production CloudKit schema (see cenno-1dn). Shipped v0.2.0 had neither profile nor icloud entitlements, so the relay was a silent no-op.

title: Produce a real Developer-ID notarized cenno.app release
description: Run scripts/release.sh with a Developer ID Application identity + App Store Connect API key so the DMG is hardened-runtime signed, notarized, and stapled (Gatekeeper-clean). Verify: codesign shows 'Developer ID Application', spctl --assess passes, stapler validate OK.

title: Ship desktop cenno via Developer-ID + notarization (not App Store)
description: Per user decision 2026-06-16: desktop macOS cenno stays off the Mac App Store (sandbox would break the MCP stdio bridge / filesystem model). Distribute as a notarized Developer-ID DMG + Homebrew + self-updater. Pipeline EXISTS (scripts/release.sh expects APPLE_SIGNING_IDENTITY='Developer ID Application: … (79SP7TM5PH)' + APPLE_API_KEY/ISSUER/KEY_PATH; bundle_dmg.sh --notarize uses notarytool submit --wait + staple). GAP: shipped /Applications/cenno.app is currently signed 'Apple Development' (not Developer ID) and NOT notarized.

title: Exclude CennoRelayTest from any shippable build
description: CennoRelayTest is a macOS dev harness with bundle id app.cenno; it must never be archived/submitted. Confirm it is not part of the CennoiPhone/CennoWatch archive schemes and stays dev-only.
close_reason: Verified: CennoRelayTest is a separate macOS target with its own scheme; CennoiPhone/CennoWatch archive schemes only build their own targets, so RelayTest is never part of an iOS/watchOS archive. No change needed.

title: Privacy labels + export compliance
description: Add ITSAppUsesNonExemptEncryption (likely false) to Info.plist to skip per-build encryption prompts. Fill App Store Connect privacy nutrition labels honestly (prompt content transits user's private CloudKit DB). Provide privacy-policy + support URLs.
notes: DONE in code: ITSAppUsesNonExemptEncryption=false added to iPhone Info.plist (via project.yml). REMAINING: fill App Store Connect privacy nutrition labels + privacy-policy/support URLs (web work).
close_reason: Privacy labels + export compliance done. (1) Export compliance: ITSAppUsesNonExemptEncryption=false now on BOTH companion + watch targets via project.yml (commit 70e8ab7) — TestFlight won't prompt per-build. (2) App Privacy nutrition label set to 'Data Not Collected' in ASC (audit confirmed no analytics/SDKs/external networking; prompt data lives in user's private CloudKit, developer has no access). (3) Privacy Policy URL set to https://cenno.salient.community/privacy (new page deployed, commit ac15fb7). NOTE: App Privacy 'Publish' button intentionally left for app-submission time (it's gated to the review flow); both inputs are saved.

title: App Store metadata + screenshots
description: Description, keywords, category, screenshots for required iPhone sizes (6.7"/6.9") and Watch sizes, app preview if any. Confirm icons present (iPhone 1024; Watch 100+1024 — a missing icon silently fails Watch install).

title: Set companion display name to 'Cenno'
description: Add CFBundleDisplayName=Cenno to iPhone + Watch Info.plist so the home-screen label reads 'Cenno' regardless of target/product name. Bundle IDs unchanged.
close_reason: Added CFBundleDisplayName=Cenno to iPhone + Watch Info.plist

title: Urgency-priority ordering in PromptRegistry::pending()
close_reason: Closed

title: Feature-factory pilot: concurrent-prompt queueing per policy
Local-file image serving for prompts (scoped asset protocol)
description: Descoped 2026-07-13: interactive image controls (select/zoom/compare, per-image reactions) are not a priority — fixed scaling stands (existing variant caps; recommended default fit:contain + mediumFeature, largeFeature when the image is the subject; documented via cenno-bee). Remaining scope here: let agents show LOCAL image files in A2UI Image — webview can't read arbitrary paths; needs deliberately scoped Tauri asset protocol (prompts are untrusted input, see SECURITY.md).

title: cenno skill: widget-advisor guide — which widget for which question
description: Docs-only addition to skills/cenno/SKILL.md (backlog 2026-07-13): a decision table mapping question types to widgets (confirm/choice/scale/slider/voice_text/a2ui media). Buildable during revenue focus.
close_reason: Widget-advisor guide added to skills/cenno/SKILL.md: decision table (question shape → widget), image conventions (data: URI + 256KiB budget + fit:contain, mediumFeature/largeFeature defaults), verified Image+ChoicePicker example fired against the live app

title: User-initiated channel — hotkey dictation → agent daemon via wait_for_user_message
description: Second wedge (docs/BACKLOG.md 2026-07-13). Global hotkey starts voice.rs dictation outside any prompt; utterance lands in a queue exposed as a long-poll MCP tool (wait_for_user_message) so any MCP-capable agent can be the brain. Cenno stays local-only; daemon holds keys/MCPs. First daemon can be 'claude -p' per utterance. Blocked-by conceptually: tell_user (responses need an outbound path).

title: sound-out: voice-picker settings UI (choose Supertonic style)
description: Live-demo feedback: user wants a settings UI to choose the TTS voice (Supertonic style F1-F5/M1-M5), rather than editing ~/.cenno tts.voice by hand. Voice is already config-driven; this is the UI. Also note: user mentioned the cenno<->spokenly dictation interaction is worth considering. (Spokenly's own popup size is out of cenno scope.)
close_reason: Settings window shipped + verified live: opens from tray, renders, reflects+writes config (no clobber — panel/widgets/routing preserved), engine/voice persist, model status shows installed, Supertonic test voice works (no fallback), output-device picker + delete-model wired.

title: sound-out: emotional/prosody markup for supporting TTS providers
description: Pass through emotional/prosody markup to providers that accept it; graceful fallback for those that don't. Depends on cloud providers. Follow-on to epic cenno-dib.

title: sound-out: ElevenLabs sound-generation earcons per urgency
description: Generate/caches earcons (alert tones) from a prompt via ElevenLabs sound-gen API; play a distinct cue per urgency before/with speech. Follow-on to epic cenno-dib.

title: sound-out: pluggable cloud TTS providers (Groq/OpenAI/Cartesia/ElevenLabs)
description: Add a provider abstraction behind tts_speak so config can select a cloud TTS provider + key (env/secrets, never committed). Privacy: only send prompt text when user explicitly configured a cloud provider. Revisit R-classification + data-handling note. Follow-on to epic cenno-dib.
notes: DEPRIORITIZED in favor of local-first: user prefers a better LOCAL model (Supertonic, see new issue) over cloud to keep privacy at R1. Cloud only if local quality proves insufficient.
